tiktok.caption: Everyone's chasing $1M to retire. That number runs out in 17 years. Here's what you actually need.

youtube_short.title: The $1M Retirement Goal Is $1.5M Short
title_alternatives: Why $1 Million Won't Actually Let You Retire
The Retirement Number Nobody Tells You About
youtube_short.description: Everyone's told to save $1 million to retire — but at 4% withdrawal, that's only $40,000 a year. The average retiree spends $57,818. Add $315,000 in healthcare costs and 20 years of inflation erasing 40% of your buying power, and the real number is closer to $2.5 million. Start at 30 and it costs $1,600 a month. Wait until 40 and it jumps to $2,900. #retirement #personalfinance #retirementplanning #moneypsychology — Follow for the money math they never taught you.
